name: Alto de Cepedelo and Pías Reservoir Loop
name_original: Viana de Bolo – vuelta por Embalse de Pías – Zona Especial de Conservación Peña Trevinca
description: Tackle the difficult Alto de Cepedelo and Pías Reservoir Loop, a 28.4-mile touring cycling route with 4065 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Viana Do Bolo traverse a landscape characterized by significant mountain ranges and deep river valleys. The region features a striking contrast between high peaks like Pena Trevinca and the valleys carved by rivers such as the Bibei, Camba, and O Vao. Cyclists will encounter a network of reservoirs, including Pías and O Vao, often surrounded by picturesque mountains, alongside old oak and chestnut forests. This diverse terrain offers varied routes for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Viana Do Bolo The most popular touring cycling route is Alto de Cepedelo and Pías Reservoir Loop, a 28.4 miles (45.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete, offering views of the Pías Reservoir and surrounding mountain scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of the Reservoir – A Venda da Teresa loop from A Gudiña-Porta de Galicia, a difficult 80.6 miles (129.8 km) path. This route provides extensive views over the region's reservoirs and mountainous terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Cruceiro de Laza – View of the Reservoir loop from A Gudiña-Porta De Galicia, a 69.8 miles (112.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with reservoir vistas, often completed in about 7 hours 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Viana Do Bolo is defined by its mountainous backdrops, deep river valleys, and numerous reservoirs. The network primarily offers challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Viana Do Bolo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Viana Do Bolo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Viana Do Bolo?
A: There are 6 touring cycling routes available around Viana Do Bolo. These routes are generally challenging, offering significant elevation gains through the region's mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: All touring cycling routes listed for Viana Do Bolo are rated as difficult. They feature substantial elevation changes, traversing mountainous backdrops and deep river valleys, making them suitable for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: You can expect a diverse and challenging terrain characterized by a striking contrast between high mountain ranges, such as Pena Trevinca, and deep river valleys carved by rivers like the Bibei, Camba, and O Vao. Routes often wind along picturesque reservoirs and through old oak and chestnut forests.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017331807/alto-de-cepedelo-and-pias-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Cepedelo and Pías Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Pías Reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Reservoir – A Venda da Teresa loop from A Gudiña-Porta de Galicia&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive views over the region's reservoirs.

Q: What natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The routes showcase the region's remarkable natural beauty. You'll encounter numerous reservoirs like Pías, Bibei, O Vao, Beo, and Valdesirgas, often surrounded by high mountains. Keep an eye out for clear mountain streams, dotted lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de Carrizais&lt;/a&gt;, and dense forests of oak and chestnut trees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bibei River Valley&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent feature.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to explore while cycling in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Yes, the region offers several historical points of interest. You can explore the charming historic town center of Viana do Bolo with its medieval streets and the Tower of Viana. Further afield, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza&lt;/a&gt; is a beautiful religious site, and the ancient Roman gold mine of As Borreas in Caldesiños provides a glimpse into the area's rich past.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Yes, you can find a notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;waterfall at Embalse de Valdesirgas&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a scenic stop during your touring cycling adventure.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: Viana Do Bolo offers numerous scenic viewpoints due to its mountainous terrain. You can enjoy panoramic vistas from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7989709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Puente Porto reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Beo Reservoir and Viana de Bolo from the San Cibrao viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chaira das Lamas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of a loop from O Barrio dos Xitanos.

Q: Is Viana Do Bolo suitable for family-friendly touring cycling?
A: Given that all listed touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo are rated as difficult with significant elevation gains, the area is generally more suited for experienced adult cyclists rather than families with young children or beginners. The terrain is quite challenging.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The mountainous nature of Viana Do Bolo suggests that late spring, summer, and early autumn are generally the most favorable seasons for touring cycling. During these months, the weather is typically milder and more stable, making the challenging routes more enjoyable. Winter can bring colder temperatures and snow to higher elevations.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the dramatic mountainous backdrops, the serene beauty of the numerous reservoirs, and the challenging yet rewarding nature of the rides through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any less crowded touring cycling routes in Viana Do Bolo?
A: While Viana Do Bolo is not a heavily trafficked cycling destination, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Cairel loop from A Veiga&lt;/a&gt;, being a bit shorter, might offer a more secluded experience compared to the longer, more popular loops. The region generally provides a peaceful environment for exploration.
tips: At an altitude of 700 m, the small town of Viana del Bollo located on the banks of the Bao Reservoir is surrounded by native Galician oak and chestnut forest. The city's history dates back to Roman times, with its famous mineral mines still in operation today.

Surrounded by high mountains on the border between Galicia and Castilla y León, the Pías reservoir is a place of great beauty. The reservoir captures water from the Bibei River to power its hydroelectric dam.
From this point close to San Cibrao, Viana de Bolo and the embalse de Beo can be seen. The water from the Bibei River is trapped in the valley by the surrounding mountains.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Alto de Cepedelo and Pías Reservoir Loop for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult. The Alto do Cepedelo climb itself is very challenging, spanning 17.3 kilometers with an accumulated elevation gain of 755 meters and maximum gradients reaching 17%. It requires very good fitness from touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily mountainous, featuring both irregular and optimal asphalt surfaces. You'll be cycling through high-altitude areas, with Cepedelo being the highest inhabited village in Galicia.

faqs.question: Is this a well-marked or established touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The 'Alto de Cepedelo and Pías Reservoir Loop' is not a widely recognized or specifically described touring cycling trail. Cyclists interested in this area will likely need to plan their own route to combine the challenging Alto do Cepedelo climb with the scenic Pías Reservoir.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the Church and Tower of Viana do Bolo, the picturesque Bibei River Valley and Pías Reservoir, and the stunning View of the Beo Reservoir and Viana de Bolo from the San Cibrao viewpoint. The high-altitude village of Cepedelo itself offers impressive mountainous scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and high altitude, the best time to cycle this route is generally during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, to avoid snow and ice. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: The route is located within the ZEPA Pena Trevinca (Special Protection Area for Birds). While there are typically no entrance fees for cycling, it's always advisable to be aware of and respect any specific regulations or conservation guidelines for protected natural areas. No special cycling permits are generally required.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is not a formally established loop, parking options would depend on your chosen starting point. Viana do Bolo, being a nearby town, would likely offer suitable parking facilities from which to begin your ride towards Alto de Cepedelo and the Pías Reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de Cepedelo and Pías Reservoir Loop suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, particularly the challenging Alto do Cepedelo climb with gradients up to 17%, this route is not recommended for families with young children or beginner touring cyclists. It requires a high level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public roads, this is a challenging cycling route on asphalt. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is accustomed to long distances, road cycling environments, and always keep it on a leash, especially in populated areas or near traffic. Consider the dog's safety and comfort on such a demanding ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Viana do Bolo, a town close to the route, offers various amenities including accommodation, cafes, and restaurants where you can refuel and rest before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while not a single established trail, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. Notably, about 40% of the route follows the challenging 'CIMA OU10 Alto de Cepedelo' climb. Smaller portions also touch upon 'A Carreira de Viana' and 'Pista da Pradeira'.
